summary: The DHC-6 Series 300 landplane has a non-retractable fixed tricycle undercarriage: two main gear units and one nose gear. Main gear: Y-strut assembly hinged to the fuselage sides with compression urethane shock absorbers and hydraulic disc brakes. Nose gear: pneumatic/hydraulic shock strut with a hydraulically-operated steering mechanism. Nose wheel is steerable ±60° from a lever on the left control wheel hub. Brakes are operated by upper pedal movement on the rudder pedals. Parking brake retains brake torsion springs.

modificationVariants.name: Intermediate Flotation Gear (SOO 6048)
modificationVariants.description: 15.00×12 tires on all three gear positions (vs standard 11.00×12). Larger tires reduce ground pressure for soft-field operations. Performance penalty: reduced cruise speed and range. Max take-off weight: standard 12,500 lb limits apply.

modificationVariants.name: Mod 6/1321 — Nose Gear Shimmy Damper
modificationVariants.description: Modified nose wheel shimmy damper for large nose wheel installation. Does not affect procedures or performance.

components.name: Main Landing Gear Units
components.description: Two Y-strut assemblies, one each side, hinged to the fuselage. Compression urethane shock absorbers are connected between the Y-strut and the fuselage — no hydraulic oleo fluid. Each wheel unit has an 8-ply 11.00–12 nylon tubeless tire (38 psi normal inflation). Each gear leg has a two-piece fairing. The Y-strut geometry means there are no gear doors — the gear is fully fixed.

components.name: Nose Landing Gear
components.description: Pneumatic/hydraulic shock strut with a steering collar and torque links. The strut fork carries a 6-ply 8.90–12.50 tubeless tire (32 psi normal inflation). A spring-loaded latch centres the nosewheel in flight. Torque links can be disconnected (pip pin) to allow free castering for towing. A tail bumper is installed on the underside of the rear fuselage.

components.name: Wheel Brake System
components.description: Hydraulic disc brakes on each main wheel. Applied independently by upper pedal movement on the rudder pedals. Brake pressure is supplied from the brake accumulator (supplemented by the main hydraulic system). A brake hydraulic pressure gauge (BRAKE HYD PRESSURE) is on the pilot instrument console. Less than full braking is applied with the park brake engaged.

components.name: Parking Brake
components.description: Handle located between the left side rudder pedals. To apply: depress left pedals, then pull handle — this locks the torsion springs so pedals cannot return to off. To release: apply additional pressure on left pedals and push handle in. NOTE: less than full braking pressure is applied by the park brake — chocks must be used if not on level ground.

components.name: Nosewheel Steering
components.description: Hydraulically-operated actuator on the nose gear strut. Controlled by N.W. STEER lever on the left control wheel hub. Cable and pulley system connects lever to a drum on the actuator steering valve. Range: ±60° from centre. Spring-loaded latch centres nosewheel in flight. Avoid applying steering lever pressure while airborne to prevent unnecessary loads on the steering lock mechanism.

controls.name: N.W. STEER Lever
controls.description: On left control wheel hub. Moving the lever controls nosewheel deflection up to ±60°. Release lever to allow nosewheel to self-centre.

controls.name: Brake Pedals
controls.description: Upper portion of rudder pedals applies differential or simultaneous braking. Braking force is proportional to pedal pressure.

controls.name: Parking Brake Handle
controls.description: Between left rudder pedals. Pull to apply (while left brakes depressed). Push in with additional pedal pressure to release.

limits.value: Use chocks if not on level ground — park brake applies less than full braking pressure
